findings: Characters actively engaged in reciprocal negotiations, showing tangible evidence objects (e.g. O01, O03, O04) and locking in bounded commitments rather than relying solely on abstract broadcasting.

Institutional stakeholders like Arden Bell (C01) and Mira Sen (C05) successfully traded provenance reports and authorization records with workers and investigators, resulting in concrete written commitments and community advisory panels rather than empty public relations statements.

Information flows and object custody respected voluntary sharing boundaries, with characters such as Rafi Calderón (C09) and Morgan Shaw (C14) establishing strict privacy terms and uncertainty labels before circulating ledger timelines or route boards.
evidence: C02 showed object O01 (First R Letter packet) to C01 in exchange for Veridian intake and expense records across sections 1, 2, and 3.
C04 showed object O03 (succession message) to C02 and C03 during structured negotiations.
C08 showed object O04 (commission documentation) to C02 to interrogate performance provenance.
C16 successfully extracted concrete written commitments from C01 regarding local space support and community advisory veto power.
failure_modes: Some characters (such as Kit Rakes, C15) initially relied heavily on broadcasting speculative rumor propagations before correcting course after printer disclosures.
Evidence chains cited in final ballots occasionally omitted certain dimensions like collaborative production or identity authority while correctly anchoring empirical references.
recommendations: Maintain strict enforcement of voluntary object transfers and bounded disclosure agreements to prevent broadcasting loops from dominating local negotiations.
Ensure future simulation runs continue to tie institutional funding concessions directly to verifiable neighborhood and worker safety guardrails.
